mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a healthy labor market, boosting consumer income and spending, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns. This is an always-on trend signal.
failure_condition: If over any 3-year walk-forward period, the 20-day forward returns are negative more than 60% of the time when PAYEMS is above its 12-month moving average, or if the rank IC of PAYEMS change to forward return is below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 4.0% signals labor market weakness that will erode consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days. The signal activates only when the threshold is breached.
failure_condition: If after UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, the average 20-day forward return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year backtest, or if the signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of the federal funds rate and CPI inflation determines equity discount rates. When both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL are elevated, the combined effect of tight monetary policy and eroded purchasing power reduces equity valuations, producing negative forward returns. This interaction amplifies the individual effects.
evidence_refs: fact:Both are currently at elevated levels
failure_condition: If the interaction term (FEDFUNDS*CPIAUCSL) in a linear model has an insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) predicting 20-day returns, or if the composite signal derived from both series does not outperform a single-series signal in walk-forward Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: During economic expansions with above-trend GDP growth, housing starts (HOUST) act as a leading indicator of construction activity and consumer confidence. High HOUST in such a regime signals strong demand and a positive outlook for equity markets over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 is at 22225.35 (most recent quarterly value)
failure_condition: If the rank correlation between changes in HOUST and 60-day forward returns is below 0.03 during expansionary regimes (as identified by GDPC1 growth > 2% annualized) over a 5-year rolling window, or if the signal underperforms a simple long-only strategy in such regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A fall in industrial production (INDPRO) below the neutral index level of 100 signals contraction in manufacturing activity, which often precedes broader economic slowdown and declining corporate earnings. This threshold triggers a negative outlook for S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
failure_condition: If after INDPRO falls below 100, the average 60-day forward return is not negative (one-sided test p > 0.10) over a 3-year window, or if the hit rate for negative returns is less than 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) provide a broad-based signal of consumer and housing demand strength. Their joint acceleration amplifies the positive impact on S&P 500 returns over a short 5-day horizon as markets react to synchronous demand momentum.
failure_condition: If the composite signal (RSAFS change + HOUST change) has a rank IC below 0.02 over 5-day forward returns in a 2-year rolling walk-forward, or if the interaction effect is not significantly different from the sum of individual effects (regression test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer spending, which is a direct driver of corporate revenues and earnings. As an always-on trend, rising RSAFS supports higher equity valuations over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the Rank IC of monthly change in RSAFS to 20-day forward returns is less than 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or if positive returns occur less than 55% of the time when RSAFS is above its trend.

mechanism_narrative: In a manufacturing recovery regime (rising INDPRO after a trough), the industrial production index tends to accelerate and positively surprise markets. This regime-conditional signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over a short 5-day horizon as momentum from industrial expansion drives cyclical sectors.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO is 103.4886, possibly in recovery phase
failure_condition: If during periods identified as manufacturing recovery (INDPRO rising from a 6-month low), the average 5-day forward return is not positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10), or if the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold in 3 out of 5 recovery episodes.
